observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer deposit the collections regularly and intact to avoid misuse, theft or loss of government funds, pursuant to Section 32 of the Manual on the NGAS.

We recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant to implement the provisions of COA Circular No. 96-011, Section 59 of LGU-NGAS, Volume 1 Chapter 3 on Spoiled and Stale Checks, and DBM Circular Letter No. 2008-8 and record adjustments for valid reconciling items.
Further, it is recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant:
Send communications to the concerned payees or creditors informing them on the status of the checks to be claimed. If the concerned payees or creditors appeared to claim the checks, the Municipal Treasurer issue a new check to the claimant; and
After exhaustion of the communication phase and there are still unclaimed checks in the possession of the Municipal Treasurer, the Municipal Accountant adjust the following accounts, as follows:
Cash in Bank				XXX
Accounts Payable/ Other Appropriate Liability Account 	XXX

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
Carry out the necessary actions to settle the long outstanding unliquidated balances of the former municipal mayor and former municipal treasurer.

We recommended that the Accounting Office:
Recognize impairment loss equivalent to the outstanding balance of dormant accounts to ensure that Accounts Receivable - Others are appropriately valued at net of impairment loss as of year-end.
Re-submit the request to COA for authority to write-off from the books those accounts considered dormant following the procedures set forth in COA Circular No. 2016-005.

We recommended to Management that:
Inventory Committee, GSO, and Municipal Accountant are to be guided by COA Circular No. 2020-006 on the process to conduct physical count of PPE, preparation of necessary reports, reconciliation procedures, and recording in the books.
Inventory Committee prepare and submit the RPCPPE upon the completion of the physical inventory.
All offices maintain a separate list or inventory of PPE under their custodianship, update property records, conduct pre-count of these PPE, and submit Departmental RPCPPE to the Inventory Committee for validation during the annual physical inventory.

We recommended that the GSO:
Organize periodic disposal of unserviceable property to prevent further deterioration and loss in recoverable value, clear out hazardous waste or materials and optimize use of storage spaces; and
Timely submit Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP).

We recommended that the GSO Designate:
Cancel the Acknowledgment Receipts of Equipment for the issued semi-expendable property and replace these with Inventory Custodian Slips (ICS).
Use the ICS for issuance of tangible items with cost below P15,000.00 and have a life of more than one year and are not considered as PPE to establish monitoring, control and accountability over them, moving forward.
Also, we recommended that the Accounting Office:
Revisit PPE accounts and reclassify the semi-expendable properties which were recognized as PPE to the appropriate inventory or expense accounts. For those items already issued to the end-users as of beginning of CY 2021, the carrying amount shall be recognized as prior period adjustment.
Apply the criteria on capitalization of PPE as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2020-006 to ensure proper classification of assets in the books.

We reiterated our recommendation that Municipal Accountant reconcile the discrepancies noted and make the necessary adjustments/ corrections in the books of accounts to reflect the correct balances of the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts.
Also, for the Municipal Accountant to adapt measures/ strategies to determine the deficiencies noted considering the lapse of time that the discrepancies were incurred.

We recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant to:

Ensure the completeness of prescribed supporting documents for the granting of cash advances in processing claims for payment thereof; 

Strictly monitor periodically the submission of liquidation reports to ensure timely accounting thereof to avoid undue accumulation of outstanding cash advances; and

Carry out necessary actions to settle the long outstanding unliquidated balances of the former municipal mayor and former municipal treasurer.

We recommended that Municipal Accountant:

apply the perpetual inventory method and the accounting standards of recording purchases of drugs, medicines, and apparatus as Inventories.
recognize the corresponding expenses for actual issuances to the units; and 
maintain supplies ledger cards. 

On the other hand, the General Services Officer maintain stock/property cards and regularly reconcile with the ledger cards of the accounting unit.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:

Submit the required supporting documents for the above-mentioned disbursements.

Examine completeness of supporting documents for subsequent disbursements to ensure compliance with the basic documentary requirements.
We recommended that management ensure that appropriation of 1% SCPWDF is properly made to avoid under-appropriation.

We recommended that Management:

a)	Make a representation with the DOF thru the BLGF-RO IX regarding the details of the required incomplete income data submitted previously and submit the same for the approval of the level and income reclassification of LGU-Bayog from 3rd class to 2nd class municipality.

b)	If no details as to required income data, inquire with the DOF thru the BLGF-RO IX about the process and documentary requirements for level and income reclassification and prepare a new request for the reclassification of LGU-Bayog from 3rd class to 2nd class municipality and ensure completeness of the required documentary requirements.   

c)	Pending approval of the reclassification from 3rd class to 2nd class municipality, adjust monthly salary rates of officials and employees based on 3rd class level and income reclassification of LGU-Bayog as provided in Annex “A-5” of DBM-LBC No. 118 and correspondingly adjust LGUs share of GSIS contribution, PhilHealth contribution and Employees Compensation Insurance.
We recommended that management ensure that the incurrence of expenditures shall be within the reasonable limits set by law, rules and regulations, as defined in COA Circular No. 2012-003.

We recommended that the BAC follow the guidelines as provided in Appendix 18 of the Handbook on Philippine Government Procurement Sixth Edition relative to Shopping and Small Value Procurement to avail the most advantageous price in favor of the municipal government.  In addition, the Municipal Accountant to examine completeness of supporting documents for every transaction of the municipality before payment shall be done to ensure validity, propriety and legality of the disbursements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC to strictly observe the procedural guidelines on the procurement process as provided under the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 to avail of the most advantageous price in favor of the municipality, as well as to ensure that procurement under an emergency basis shall be made only under the circumstances enumerated therein.

We recommended that the BAC observe the guidelines set by the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and GPPB Resolution No. 05-2018 in procuring POL to avoid splitting of contracts. Further, procurement of POL should be based on the APP and PPMP to ensure that each quarter fuel requirements will be procured thru competitive bidding.

We recommended that Management thru the Municipal Engineer and Municipal Accountant make representation with the concerned suppliers and contractors to post warranty securities for the purchase of goods and equipment and infrastructure projects that had already been fully paid. Henceforth, strictly observe the mandatory posting of warranty security to guarantee that the contractor shall perform his responsibilities as prescribed in Section 62.1 and 62.2.3.1 of IRR of R.A. 9184 and Section 9.1.1.4 and 9.1.3.1 of COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

Further for management to verify the authenticity of the Warranty Bond/Surety Bond before releasing the retention fee to the contractor.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended the following:

a)	Require BAC to observe the provisions of 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 in the posting of Notice of Award, Notice to Proceed and the approved contract in the PhilGEPS website. Moreover, require the BAC Secretariat to issue certification on the posting of the aforementioned documents in any conspicuous places in the premises of the Procuring Entity;

b)	Require the Municipal Accountant to refrain from processing payment to contractor without the required posting by the BAC of the notice of award, notice to proceed and approved contract in the PhilGEPS website and certification from the BAC on the posting of the aforementioned documents in any conspicuous places in the premises of the Procuring Entity; and

c)	Submit evidence of posting of Notice of Award and Notice to Proceed in the PhilGEPS website of the said procurements.

We recommended that the General Services Officer (GSO) - Designate issue ARE for all equipment issued for the official use of officials and employees.  Also, the Municipal Accountant require submission of ARE for all non-expendable property and equipment as required under COA Circular No. 2012-001 after payment has been made and attached the ARE to the disbursement voucher.

We recommended that the local chief executive and head of the legislative department and its members coordinate for the formulation and establishment of internal guidelines on the granting of financial assistance on burial, medical and other emergency needs for the proper and efficient utilization of the AICS program.

We recommended that Management observe the specific guidelines on the grant of honoraria set forth by DBM Budget Circular No. 2004-5A. Further, payment of honoraria claims of BAC members, secretariat and TWG be duly supported with documentary requirements in compliance with COA Circular 2012-001.
We recommended that management ensure that fund transfers to implementing agency are supported with complete documentation pursuant to COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

We recommended the following:

a)Local Chief Executive ensures the full utilization of the 20% EDF in compliance with the responsibilities provided for in paragraph 5.0 of DILG and DBM JMC No. 2017-1 to achieve desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes.  

b)MPDC as head of the secretariat of the MDC ensures that the identified PPAs are properly selected, planned and evaluated.  

c)MDC requests the technical assistance of the Municipal Engineer in the preparation of the estimated cost for each PPAs identified to avoid over-appropriation. 

d)Members of the MDC coordinate, monitor and evaluate the implementation of development programs and projects to ensure implementation of PPAs as required in Section 109 of RA 7160.

e)Consider the reprogramming of the balances of all implemented PAPs into new PAPs.

We recommended that Management require the officials concerned to strictly adhere to Annex E, Section 6 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184, and deduct retention money from every progress payment until fifty percent (50%) of the value of works.

Moreover, management ensure that net income is generated for each LEEs before allowing any transfer of surplus to the General Fund-Proper.

Further for management to verify the authenticity of the Warranty Bond/Surety Bond before releasing the retention fee to the contractor.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended the following:
Require BAC to observe the provisions of 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 in the posting of Notice of Award, Notice to Proceed and the approved contract in the PhilGEPS website. Moreover, require the BAC Secretariat to issue certification on the posting of the aforementioned documents in any conspicuous places in the premises of the Procuring Entity;
Require the Municipal Accountant to refrain from processing payment to contractor without the required posting by the BAC of the notice of award, notice to proceed and approved contract in the PhilGEPS website and certification from the BAC on the posting of the aforementioned documents in any conspicuous places in the premises of the Procuring Entity; and
Submit evidence of posting of Notice of Award and Notice to Proceed in the PhilGEPS website of the said procurements.

We recommended that the local chief executive and head of the legislative department and its members coordinate for the formulation and establishment of internal guidelines on the granting of financial assistance on burial, medical and other emergency needs for the proper and efficient utilization of the AICS program.

We recommended that management ensure that fund transfers to implementing agency are supported with complete documentation pursuant to COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

We recommended the following:
Local Chief Executive ensures the full utilization of the 20% EDF in compliance with the responsibilities provided for in paragraph 5.0 of DILG and DBM JMC No. 2017-1 to achieve desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes.
MPDC as head of the secretariat of the MDC ensures that the identified PPAs are properly selected, planned and evaluated.
MDC requests the technical assistance of the Municipal Engineer in the preparation of the estimated cost for each PPAs identified to avoid over-appropriation.
Members of the MDC coordinate, monitor and evaluate the implementation of development programs and projects to ensure implementation of PPAs as required in Section 109 of RA 7160.
Consider the reprogramming of the balances of all implemented PAPs into new PAPs.

We recommended that Management ensure compliance with the budgetary PS limitations as provided for under Sec. 325(a) of R.A. 7160:
Refrain from providing appropriation for unfilled positions (except for those unfilled positions that require appropriation by law).
Use the accurate level and income classification of the Municipality in the   implementation of compensation adjustment.
Refrain from granting Year End Bonus and Cash Gift if will result to excess PS over the limitation.
Institute a strong internal control over the preparation, enactment and approval of the PS expenditures in the annual budget and supplemental budget to ensure that the Municipality has a balance budget and that funds are allocated to the delivery of basic services and development projects.

We recommended that Management require the officials concerned to strictly adhere to Annex E, Section 6 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184, and deduct retention money from every progress payment until fifty percent (50%) of the value of works.

We recommended that management:
Demand from the contractor the immediate completion of the construction of the four projects;
Direct the MEO to take overall considerations that will be needed in making plans for the projects; and
Require the contractor to pay liquidated damages. If the contractor has not been paid the remaining contract price, deduct the liquidated damages from such amount.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer deposits the collections regularly and intact to avoid misuse, theft or loss of government funds, pursuant to Section 32 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System.
It is recommended that the Municipal Treasurer immediately submits the Disbursement Vouchers of unrecorded withdrawals to cause immediate adjustments in the books.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant implements the provisions of COA Circular No. 96-011, Section 59 of LGU-NGAS Volume 1 Chapter 3 on Spoiled and Stale Checks, and DBM Circular Letter No. 2008-8 and record adjustments for valid reconciling items.
Further, it is recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant:
Send communications to the concerned payees or creditors informing them on the status of the checks to be claimed. If the concerned payees or creditors appeared to claim the checks, the Municipal Treasurer issued a new check to the claimant; and
After exhaustion of the communication phase and there were still unclaimed checks in the possession of the Municipal Treasurer, the Municipal Accountant adjust the following accounts;
Cash in Bank					     XXX
Accounts Payable/ Other Appropriate Liability Account 	XXX

We recommended that the officers and employees follow the guidelines on travel under EO 298 and Section 96 of RA 7160. We further recommended the Municipal Accountant to examine the completeness of the supporting documents to avoid suspension/disallowance of disbursements in audit.

We recommended that the management:
● ceases from granting additional cash advances to officers/employees with unsettled cash advances;
● sends immediate demand letters to employees with outstanding cash advances and require   them to immediately settle the amount granted. If liquidation is still not achieved, consider withholding their salaries or any amount due them until balances have been fully settled; and
● requires the Accountant to monitor outstanding cash advances of every officials & employees and report the status of their liquidations to management.

We recommended that the General Services Officer as the accountable person for the property, plant, and equipment and the Municipal Accountant reconcile the discrepancies between the inventory reports and accounting records.

We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer in approving Obligation Request (ObR) for obligations charged against LDRRMF ensures that the same is in accordance with the guidelines set under Paragraphs 5.1 to 5.4 of Section 5 of the Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013 of the NDRRMC, DBM and DILG.
On the other hand, the LCE approve only the procurement within the sample lists of the MC 2012-73 of the DILG such that the 70% of the 5% may be utilized only to procure early warning systems, preparedness equipment and other equipage and in relation to the corresponding activities under the four aspects such as:    (a) prevention and mitigation (b) preparedness (c) response and (d) reconstruction and rehabilitation.
Also, the MDRRM Office request a budget/ appropriation in the GF proper necessary in the conduct of their day to day operations in their Office since the Circular clearly emphasizes the utilization of LDRRMF for disaster reduction, preparedness and mitigation measures.

We recommended that the LCE, the LDC, and the Local Finance Committee be guided by the limitations set forth in the JMC No. 2011-01 in the preparation of the budget for 20% DF and shall refrain from allocating and disbursing funds for programs and activities that are inappropriate to be charged against the 20% DF.

We recommended that the management strictly implements the provisions of DSWD Order No. 22, as amended by AO No. 5, Series of 2008, and Section 4(2 & 6) of PD 1445. This recommendation is without prejudice to the issuance of Notices of Suspension and/or Disallowance resulting from post-audit of transactions.
We further recommended that management refrain from granting financial assistance to support the activities of the various organizations which are not falling within the mandate of the municipal government.

We recommended that the BAC follow the provisions in Section 21.2.1of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 to achieve transparency and competitiveness in government procurement. The Municipal Accountant shall ensure completeness of supporting documents before payment is made.
